task.summary: Update default Android package name in Fastlane Appfile
chore: add nature and animal presets, first 3 example prefabs

feat: wip 1
prompt: You are working in a fresh Unity URP project.
Set up a clean folder architecture for a small children’s 3D biome exploration game.
Create only scripts and ScriptableObject definitions. Do not assume specific third-party asset names yet.
Create: - Assets/Game/Scripts/BiomeDefinition.cs - Assets/Game/Scripts/AnimalDefinition.cs - Assets/Game/Scripts/AnimalWander.cs - Assets/Game/Scripts/ClickableAnimal.cs - Assets/Game/Scripts/SimpleOrbitCamera.cs - Assets/Game/Editor/BiomeSceneGenerator.cs
The goal is a data-driven workflow where a designer assigns prefabs/materials in ScriptableObjects, then uses Tools > Generate Biome Scene to create a pleasant test scene.
Use Unity 6 / URP-compatible APIs where possible. Keep scripts simple, robust, and readable. Add null checks and helpful Debug.LogWarning messages.

feat: wip 1
prompt: You are working in a fresh Unity URP project.
Set up a clean folder architecture for a small children’s 3D biome exploration game.
Create only scripts and ScriptableObject definitions. Do not assume specific third-party asset names yet.
Create: - Assets/Game/Scripts/BiomeDefinition.cs - Assets/Game/Scripts/AnimalDefinition.cs - Assets/Game/Scripts/AnimalWander.cs - Assets/Game/Scripts/ClickableAnimal.cs - Assets/Game/Scripts/SimpleOrbitCamera.cs - Assets/Game/Editor/BiomeSceneGenerator.cs
The goal is a data-driven workflow where a designer assigns prefabs/materials in ScriptableObjects, then uses Tools > Generate Biome Scene to create a pleasant test scene.
Use Unity 6 / URP-compatible APIs where possible. Keep scripts simple, robust, and readable. Add null checks and helpful Debug.LogWarning messages.

feat: wip 2
prompt: Implement BiomeDefinition as a ScriptableObject.
It should contain: - biomeName - groundMaterial - terrainSize - treePrefabs list - rockPrefabs list - plantPrefabs list - animalDefinitions list - treeCount - rockCount - plantCount - animalGroupCount - skyColor - fogColor - fogDensity - directionalLightColor - directionalLightIntensity - ambientColor
Also implement AnimalDefinition as a ScriptableObject containing: - animalName - animalPrefab - groupSizeMin - groupSizeMax - movementRadius - moveSpeed - factText - optional AudioClip animalSound
Use attributes such as Header, Min, Range, Tooltip where useful.

feat: wip 2
prompt: Implement BiomeSceneGenerator as a Unity Editor tool.
Requirements: - Add menu item Tools > Biomes > Generate Scene From Selected Biome - It should require that a BiomeDefinition asset is selected in the Project window - Create a new empty scene - Create a large ground plane scaled to biome.terrainSize - Assign biome.groundMaterial - Scatter trees, rocks, and plants randomly on the ground - Use deterministic random seed exposed in the generator code - Avoid placing objects too close to the center spawn point - Spawn animal groups from AnimalDefinition entries - Add a main camera - Add a directional light - Configure RenderSettings fog, ambient light, and sky color - Create an empty GameObject called World - Parent generated objects under World/Trees, World/Rocks, World/Plants, World/Animals - Save the scene to Assets/Game/Scenes/Generated_<biomeName>.unity
The generated scene should look full and appealing, not empty. Use Undo.RegisterCreatedObjectUndo where appropriate. Add clear error messages.

feat: wip 3
prompt: Implement BiomeSceneGenerator as a Unity Editor tool.
Requirements: - Add menu item Tools > Biomes > Generate Scene From Selected Biome - It should require that a BiomeDefinition asset is selected in the Project window - Create a new empty scene - Create a large ground plane scaled to biome.terrainSize - Assign biome.groundMaterial - Scatter trees, rocks, and plants randomly on the ground - Use deterministic random seed exposed in the generator code - Avoid placing objects too close to the center spawn point - Spawn animal groups from AnimalDefinition entries - Add a main camera - Add a directional light - Configure RenderSettings fog, ambient light, and sky color - Create an empty GameObject called World - Parent generated objects under World/Trees, World/Rocks, World/Plants, World/Animals - Save the scene to Assets/Game/Scenes/Generated_<biomeName>.unity
The generated scene should look full and appealing, not empty. Use Undo.RegisterCreatedObjectUndo where appropriate. Add clear error messages.

feat: wip 4
prompt: Implement AnimalWander.
Behavior: - Animal wanders inside a radius around its starting position - It picks a random destination - It smoothly rotates toward movement direction - It moves at configurable speed - It waits briefly after reaching a destination - It should work without NavMesh - It should keep y position stable - It should expose movementRadius, moveSpeed, idleTimeMin, idleTimeMax - Draw a gizmo circle showing movement radius when selected
Keep it child-friendly and calm: no chasing, no fleeing, no combat.

feat: wip 5
prompt: Implement ClickableAnimal.
Behavior: - When clicked/tapped, show a simple UI fact card - Fact card displays animalName and factText - If animalSound exists, play it - Create the UI automatically at runtime if it does not already exist - UI should be readable and child-friendly - Add a close button - Make the card appear near bottom center of the screen - Support mouse click for desktop - Support touch tap for mobile - Use Unity UI, not UI Toolkit - Keep styling simple but pleasant

MESSAGE.value:
Replace StandaloneInputModule with InputSystemUIInputModule in all scenes
The project uses Unity's new Input System package but EventSystems were using the old StandaloneInputModule, causing all UI buttons to be unresponsive. Replaced in MainMenu, AnimalSelection, and GameWorld.
Add AI agent configuration (AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, .claude/settings.json)
Add AI agent configuration (AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, .claude/settings.json)
chore: ai infra up

Redesign MainMenu layout and add procedural gradient background
Rewrites MainMenuUI layout entirely in code: title anchored top with large gold text, full-width touch-friendly buttons (560x100) centred on screen, proper version label bottom-right. Adds MenuBackground which generates a midnight-blue → forest-green → dark-earth gradient texture at runtime with no external art assets.

Redesign MainMenu layout and add procedural gradient background
Rewrites MainMenuUI layout entirely in code: title anchored top with large gold text, full-width touch-friendly buttons (560x100) centred on screen, proper version label bottom-right. Adds MenuBackground which generates a midnight-blue → forest-green → dark-earth gradient texture at runtime with no external art assets.

Style AnimalSelection scene: dark theme, gold cards, preview panel
ApplyTheme() runs in Awake before Start builds the card list: sets camera to dark midnight-forest background, makes scroll area transparent, styles preview panel dark, all labels gold/white, search field and filter buttons consistent with MainMenu palette. AnimalCardUI styles each card dark with gold name on instantiation.

Style AnimalSelection scene: dark theme, gold cards, preview panel
ApplyTheme() runs in Awake before Start builds the card list: sets camera to dark midnight-forest background, makes scroll area transparent, styles preview panel dark, all labels gold/white, search field and filter buttons consistent with MainMenu palette. AnimalCardUI styles each card dark with gold name on instantiation.

Fix TMP compile errors: MidpointLeft and enableWordWrapping
TextAlignmentOptions.MidpointLeft does not exist; replaced with MidlineLeft. enableWordWrapping is obsolete in TMP; replaced with textWrappingMode = TextWrappingModes.Normal.

Fix TMP compile errors: MidpointLeft and enableWordWrapping
TextAlignmentOptions.MidpointLeft does not exist; replaced with MidlineLeft. enableWordWrapping is obsolete in TMP; replaced with textWrappingMode = TextWrappingModes.Normal.

Wire titleText and MenuBackground in MainMenu scene
Connects TitleText GameObject to the new titleText field on MainMenuUI. Adds MenuBackground component to the Background Image for the procedural gradient. Both set manually in the Unity Inspector.

Wire titleText and MenuBackground in MainMenu scene
Connects TitleText GameObject to the new titleText field on MainMenuUI. Adds MenuBackground component to the Background Image for the procedural gradient. Both set manually in the Unity Inspector.

Fix UI layout: fill parent RectTransform and add gradient background for URP
- MainMenuUI: force RectTransform to stretch-fill Canvas so button anchor positions calculate against the full screen; move buttons to y=-160/-300 (center-relative) and subtext to match - AnimalSelectionUI: same stretch-fill fix; replace Camera.backgroundColor (ignored by URP) with a programmatic gradient Image panel injected as first sibling so the background is always visible

Fix UI layout: fill parent RectTransform and add gradient background for URP
- MainMenuUI: force RectTransform to stretch-fill Canvas so button anchor positions calculate against the full screen; move buttons to y=-160/-300 (center-relative) and subtext to match - AnimalSelectionUI: same stretch-fill fix; replace Camera.backgroundColor (ignored by URP) with a programmatic gradient Image panel injected as first sibling so the background is always visible
chore: add nature and animal presets, first 3 example prefabs

feat: wip 1
prompt: You are working in a fresh Unity URP project.
Set up a clean folder architecture for a small children’s 3D biome exploration game.
Create only scripts and ScriptableObject definitions. Do not assume specific third-party asset names yet.
Create: - Assets/Game/Scripts/BiomeDefinition.cs - Assets/Game/Scripts/AnimalDefinition.cs - Assets/Game/Scripts/AnimalWander.cs - Assets/Game/Scripts/ClickableAnimal.cs - Assets/Game/Scripts/SimpleOrbitCamera.cs - Assets/Game/Editor/BiomeSceneGenerator.cs
The goal is a data-driven workflow where a designer assigns prefabs/materials in ScriptableObjects, then uses Tools > Generate Biome Scene to create a pleasant test scene.
Use Unity 6 / URP-compatible APIs where possible. Keep scripts simple, robust, and readable. Add null checks and helpful Debug.LogWarning messages.

feat: wip 2
prompt: Implement BiomeDefinition as a ScriptableObject.
It should contain: - biomeName - groundMaterial - terrainSize - treePrefabs list - rockPrefabs list - plantPrefabs list - animalDefinitions list - treeCount - rockCount - plantCount - animalGroupCount - skyColor - fogColor - fogDensity - directionalLightColor - directionalLightIntensity - ambientColor
Also implement AnimalDefinition as a ScriptableObject containing: - animalName - animalPrefab - groupSizeMin - groupSizeMax - movementRadius - moveSpeed - factText - optional AudioClip animalSound
Use attributes such as Header, Min, Range, Tooltip where useful.
chore: ai repo setup

feat: wip 3
prompt: Implement BiomeSceneGenerator as a Unity Editor tool.
Requirements: - Add menu item Tools > Biomes > Generate Scene From Selected Biome - It should require that a BiomeDefinition asset is selected in the Project window - Create a new empty scene - Create a large ground plane scaled to biome.terrainSize - Assign biome.groundMaterial - Scatter trees, rocks, and plants randomly on the ground - Use deterministic random seed exposed in the generator code - Avoid placing objects too close to the center spawn point - Spawn animal groups from AnimalDefinition entries - Add a main camera - Add a directional light - Configure RenderSettings fog, ambient light, and sky color - Create an empty GameObject called World - Parent generated objects under World/Trees, World/Rocks, World/Plants, World/Animals - Save the scene to Assets/Game/Scenes/Generated_<biomeName>.unity
The generated scene should look full and appealing, not empty. Use Undo.RegisterCreatedObjectUndo where appropriate. Add clear error messages.

feat: wip 3
prompt: Implement BiomeSceneGenerator as a Unity Editor tool.
Requirements: - Add menu item Tools > Biomes > Generate Scene From Selected Biome - It should require that a BiomeDefinition asset is selected in the Project window - Create a new empty scene - Create a large ground plane scaled to biome.terrainSize - Assign biome.groundMaterial - Scatter trees, rocks, and plants randomly on the ground - Use deterministic random seed exposed in the generator code - Avoid placing objects too close to the center spawn point - Spawn animal groups from AnimalDefinition entries - Add a main camera - Add a directional light - Configure RenderSettings fog, ambient light, and sky color - Create an empty GameObject called World - Parent generated objects under World/Trees, World/Rocks, World/Plants, World/Animals - Save the scene to Assets/Game/Scenes/Generated_<biomeName>.unity
The generated scene should look full and appealing, not empty. Use Undo.RegisterCreatedObjectUndo where appropriate. Add clear error messages.

feat: wip 4
prompt: Implement AnimalWander.
Behavior: - Animal wanders inside a radius around its starting position - It picks a random destination - It smoothly rotates toward movement direction - It moves at configurable speed - It waits briefly after reaching a destination - It should work without NavMesh - It should keep y position stable - It should expose movementRadius, moveSpeed, idleTimeMin, idleTimeMax - Draw a gizmo circle showing movement radius when selected
Keep it child-friendly and calm: no chasing, no fleeing, no combat.

feat: wip 5
prompt: Implement ClickableAnimal.
Behavior: - When clicked/tapped, show a simple UI fact card - Fact card displays animalName and factText - If animalSound exists, play it - Create the UI automatically at runtime if it does not already exist - UI should be readable and child-friendly - Add a close button - Make the card appear near bottom center of the screen - Support mouse click for desktop - Support touch tap for mobile - Use Unity UI, not UI Toolkit - Keep styling simple but pleasant
chor: add license
